reason: Setting umask in /etc/bashrc is a standard hardening step that only affects default file permissions for newly created files in bash sessions. It does not modify authentication, networking, or critical system files, and poses minimal risk of lockout or service disruption.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Setting an overly restrictive umask could prevent users from writing to their own files; Setting an overly permissive umask could lead to unintentionally accessible files
